feed.title: NASA
feed.subtitle: Combined NASA feed: the main newsroom, the blogs firehose, NASA Science, the launch schedule, the Image of the Day, and Astronomy Picture of the Day.

entry.title: Shadow and Rainbow
entry.content: At sunset, an alignment of rainbow and mountain shadow was captured in this remarkable snapshot
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
entry.content: NASA researchers recently put a new wing design, appearing long and thin with a lightweight structural design, through a series of grueling tests to find its structural limits. What they found left them encouraged about the wing’s potential, even when they pushed it past its intended limits. The 15-foot Structural Wing Experiment Evaluating Truss-bracing (SWEET-15) […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
entry.content: Description This composite of images taken by NASA’s Psyche mission shows the crescent of Mars grow as the spacecraft approached the planet for a gravity assist from May 2 to May 15, 2026. The series begins with the smallest crescent at the center of the of the image as Mars is farthest from the spacecraft, and progressively […] The post The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
entry.content: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) initiated a working group to review updated VTE case information, additional data gathered revealing altered blood flow status within a cohort of astronauts, and discuss progress of research and clinical activities intended to mitigate the risk of VTE during spaceflight with new […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Viking Mission to Mars
entry.content: On July 20, 1976, NASA's Viking 1 landed successfully on Mars and sent back to Earth the first photograph from the surface of another planet. Viking 1 was joined on the surface by twin lander Viking 2, and both were supported by Viking 1 and 2 orbiters, which also did their own science from space. The post NASA’s Viking Mission to Mars appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
entry.content: CONTRACT RELEASE NASA has selected Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC to provide comprehensive operations, maintenance, and repair services for NASA’s Ames Research Center in California’s Silicon Valley. The Ames Facilities Support Services II contract ensures that the center’s historic and specialized facilities are properly maintained, fully operational, and capable of supporting the agency’s missions and tenant partners in the NASA Research Park. The hybrid contract has a five-year period [
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
entry.content: This composite image, released on July 9, 2026, shows the region around a pulsar – a neutron star with a strong magnetic field that spins incredibly fast – within the Lighthouse nebula. The image contains X-ray data from NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory in purple, X-rays from NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) in blue, and […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
entry.content: Read this release in English here. Ya está abierto el plazo de acreditación de los medios de comunicación para el lanzamiento de la misión del telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la NASA. El lanzamiento del telescopio Roman está programado para no antes de las 7:20 a.m. (hora del este) del domingo 30 de agosto, […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Water Resources
entry.content: Supporting sustainable and adaptable water resources management. The post Water Resources appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Health and Air Quality
entry.content: Encourages the use of Earth observations in air quality management and public health. The post Health and Air Quality appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Energy and Infrastructure
entry.content: Providing solar, meteorological, and other types of Earth information for power systems planning, energy efficiency and sustainability, and resilient infrastructure. The post Energy and Infrastructure appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Ecological Conservation
entry.content: Promotes the use of Earth data to analyze and forecast changes to ecosystems and develop effective resource management strategies. The post Ecological Conservation appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Disasters
entry.content: Advancing science to reduce disaster impacts on lives, livelihoods, and critical systems. The post Disasters appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: EarthRISE
entry.content: Earth Resources for Industry and State Empowerment (EarthRISE) builds community resilience. The post EarthRISE appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI) for Applications Development
entry.content: Advancing the application of geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) for decision support. The post Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI) for Applications Development appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Wildland Fire
entry.content: Fostering the development of technology and the use of Earth data to improve understanding of wildland fires. The post Wildland Fire appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA SmallSat LEARN Forum – Upcoming Session Agenda
entry.content: SmallSat LEARN ForumS3VI HomeLEARN OverviewUpcoming SessionContact UsSession Archive NASA SmallSat LEARN Forum – Upcoming Session Interested in attending the LEARN Forum? Participation in the SmallSat LEARN Forum is by invitation only. If you are affiliated with a NASA-funded SmallSat mission and are interested in participating, please connect with us via the link below. We look […] The post NASA SmallSat LEARN Forum – Upcoming Session Agenda appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
entry.content: Media accreditation now is open for the launch of NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the agency’s SpaceX Crew-13 missions, both targeting launch in the coming months. The Roman telescope is slated to launch no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT Sunday, Aug. 30, aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A at […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
entry.content: Description Captured by the multispectral imager instrument on NASA’s Psyche mission, this is an enhanced-color mosaic created from four individual images acquired on May 15, 2026, during the spacecraft’s flyby of Mars. Psyche was traveling from right to left (northeast to southwest on Mars) during the six minutes that it took to acquire the images for […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Delivers Mars Flyby Data, Time-lapse Video
entry.content: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft flew by Mars on May 15, using the planet’s gravity to gain speed and slightly tilt its trajectory. The flyby also gave the team an opportunity to prep for the science they will be conducting when they reach the metal-rich asteroid Psyche in 2029. Using Mars as a stand-in for the asteroid […] The post NASA’s Psyche Mission Delivers Mars Flyby Data, Time-lapse Video appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Week Wraps with New Crew Adjustments and Human Research Studies
entry.content: Crew swap activities are underway aboard the International Space Station as three new crewmates continue getting up to speed with living and working in microgravity and another Expedition 74 trio prepares to return to Earth. Meanwhile, scientific operations are back in full swing aboard the orbital outpost with lab residents studying how weightlessness affects the human body.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Research Opportunity: Help Uncover How Venous Blood Clots Form in Space
entry.content: Research Opportunity: Help Uncover How Venous Blood Clots Form in Space The NASA-funded Translational Research Institute for Space Health (TRISH) is accepting proposals to address venous thromboembolism (VTE), a critical risk to human health during spaceflight. VTE is the formation of blood clots in veins that could lead to serious medical complications. The underlying mechanisms […] The post Research Opportunity: Help Uncover How Venous Blood Clots Form in Space appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-791 c
entry.content: TOI-791 c is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 18.6 Earths, it takes 232 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.863 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-791 c appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-6884 b
entry.content: TOI-6884 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 26.32 Jupiters, it takes 4.8 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.0629 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-6884 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-791 b
entry.content: TOI-791 b is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 9.5 Earths, it takes 139.3 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.602 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-791 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-1533 c
entry.content: TOI-1533 c is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 36.6 Earths, it takes 8.1 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.076 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-1533 c appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-1533 b
entry.content: TOI-1533 b is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 28.1 Earths, it takes 3.6 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.0445 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-1533 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: What’s a Chase Plane and Why Does the X-59 Need One?
entry.content: You may have noticed that NASA’s X-59 has a shadow – another aircraft trailing it from a safe distance. That sidekick is known as a “chase plane,” and it has an important role in NASA’s Quesst mission. Chase planes are commonly used during the flight testing of major experimental aircraft, providing an extra layer of observation and support for test pilots. […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
entry.content: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the global coalition committed to responsible space exploration, becoming the newest signatory and seventh African country to join the Artemis Accords. NASA’s Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson contributed video remarks for a signing ceremony on Friday, in the island nation’s city of Ébène. “We are honored to welcome Mauritius […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: TOI-6884
entry.content: The post TOI-6884 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-1533
entry.content: The post TOI-1533 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Artemis II Lunar Science
entry.content: We answer your questions from Threads! HWHAP celebrates nine years of podcasting by bringing on Dr. Kelsey Young to discuss the Artemis II lunar science team, and the crew's lunar flyby observations and photo targets. The post Artemis II Lunar Science appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Earth Action
entry.content: Accelerating the use of Earth observation data for decision-making. The post Earth Action appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: The Dust Trail of Comet Tempel 2
entry.content: Comet 10P/Tempel 2 orbits the Sun once every 5
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
entry.content: The expansive mudflats, sandy beaches, and mangrove forests of the Bijagós archipelago support an array of migratory shorebirds and large numbers of sea turtles.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: AGN SIG Spotlight Series, July 21, 2026
entry.content: AGN Enthusiasts, we invite everyone to join us for the next AGN SIG Spotlight Series on July 21st at 1:00pm ET/10:00am PT. The post AGN SIG Spotlight Series, July 21, 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: DGCE SIG Seminar, July 23, 2026
entry.content: From Micro to Macro: Connecting the Physics of Galaxy Formation Across Scales. Speaker: Drummond Fielding, New York University The post DGCE SIG Seminar, July 23, 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: AMMB STIG Seminar Recording Posted
entry.content: Astrophysics from the Moon, Mars, and Beyond STIG Seminar Recording Posted The post AMMB STIG Seminar Recording Posted appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: DRAFT SSERVI Cooperative Agreement Notice Released
entry.content: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Science Mission Directorate (SMD) and Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate (HSMD) have released the draft of the fifth Solar System Exploration Research Virtual Institute (SSERVI) Cooperative Agreement Notice (CAN-5) as NNH26ZDA013J for public comment. The post DRAFT SSERVI Cooperative Agreement Notice Released appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Welcomes Serbia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
entry.content: The Republic of Serbia signed the Artemis Accords Thursday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency’s Headquarters in Washington, becoming the 69th nation to join a large community of like-minded nations committed to the peaceful, transparent, and responsible exploration of space. “Serbia’s connection to NASA reaches back to the Apollo program, when the […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Taps SpaceX’s Starlink to Deliver Artemis III Imagery from Orion
entry.content: NASA has selected SpaceX to deliver laser communications capabilities for next year’s Artemis III mission, enabling the agency to provide a front row seat for viewers from the Orion spacecraft as four astronauts test rendezvous and docking capabilities between Orion and test versions of commercial human landing systems needed to return astronauts to the Moon in 2028. To supplement Orion’s existing communications system, NASA will install two of SpaceX’s Starlink mini laser terminals – the same
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Releases Landsat 10 Spacecraft Request for Proposal
entry.content: The Landsat 10 Spacecraft Request for Proposal (RFP) is available for review via SAM.gov as of Jul 14, 2026. Proposals are due Aug 13, 2026 at 1:00 PM EDT. The post NASA Releases Landsat 10 Spacecraft Request for Proposal appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Expanded Crew Works Science, Microgravity Adaptation, and Next Crew Departure
entry.content: The International Space Station is a busy place hosting ten crewmates from NASA, Roscosmos, and ESA (European Space Agency) through the end of July. The expanded Expedition 74 crew is continuing its microgravity research, helping three new arrivals adjust to life in space, and preparing for the departure of another three crew members. NASA flight […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Key Aqua Project Personnel
entry.content: Meet the team behind NASA's Aqua satellite mission. The post Key Aqua Project Personnel appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Study Finds Near-Earth Asteroid Is Actually Comet
entry.content: New research led by scientists at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California has revealed the identity of a puzzling near-Earth object by precisely tracking its motion through space and using powerful observatories that image faint celestial objects. This object has a dual personality: Past images hadn’t revealed obvious cometlike activity, suggesting it might be […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Telescope Milestone: Teams Check Out NASA Roman Solar Panels
entry.content: The solar panels that will help power NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Telescope completed prelaunch cleaning and inspections July 8 at the Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida. Engineers and technicians are following necessary procedures to protect Roman from tiny debris, dust, or other material so that it operates optimally in […] The post Telescope Milestone: Teams Check Out NASA Roman Solar Panels appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Viking: 50 Years on Mars — Poster
entry.content: Throughout history, Mars had been in sight, but never within reach — until July 20, 1976, when NASA’s Viking 1 lander touched down safely and began transmitting the first images from the surface of the Red Planet. Its twin, Viking 2, arrived a few weeks later, on Sept. 3, 1976. Each of the landers was […] The post Viking: 50 Years on Mars — Poster appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Dr. Tahani Amer
entry.content: Dr. Tahani Amer is a distinguished NASA leader with over three decades of experience in engineering, science, program management, and strategic leadership across federal and private sectors. She currently serves as the Acting Associate Director for Flight Programs in NASA’s Astrophysics Division (APD), where she provides executive oversight of APD’s diverse portfolio of flight missions, […] The post Dr. Tahani Amer appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Real World: NASA’s Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Mission
entry.content: Join NASA scientists for a look at the new Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO). Find out about the instruments that will make a comprehensive map of the moon and search for safe landing sites by collecting unprecedented amounts of data. Instructionally Relevant Some details are dated. This video describes NASA’s plans to launch the Lunar Reconnaissance […] The post Real World: NASA’s Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Mission appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Young Galaxy Cluster
entry.content: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope takes us 4.4 billion years in the past with this July 3, 2026, image of a young galaxy cluster, MACS J0553.4-3342. The cluster is composed of two actively merging sub-clusters, roughly equal in mass. Each sub-cluster is anchored on an immensely bright and massive elliptical galaxy, easily identifiable as the […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NGC 300: A Cosmic Gemstone with Stars and Gas Clouds
entry.content: This sparkling, colorful gemstone is a spiral galaxy, NGC 300
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Ontario Wildfire Smoke Moves East
entry.content: Canadian wildfires sent plumes of smoke streaming over Ontario, Quebec, and parts of the U.S. Midwest and Northeast.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Uses Subscale Aircraft to Accelerate Flight Innovation
entry.content: Testing new aerospace concepts in flight remains one of NASA’s most effective ways to advance knowledge and reduce risk. The Dale Reed Subscale Flight Research Laboratory at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, supports this mission by using small, remotely piloted and autonomous aircraft as cost‑effective platforms to mature innovative ideas, accelerate learning, […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: GW SIG Seminar, 23 July 2026
entry.content: GW SIG Seminar on LISA Preparatory Science and LISA Science Gaps. Details TBA The post GW SIG Seminar, 23 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Spacecraft Commissioning On Track for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift
entry.content: Teams continue to progress through in-orbit preparations for the robotic servicing spacecraft designed to boost NASA’s Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory to a higher altitude.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Study of Pristine Meteorite Adds to Story of Ancient Asteroids
entry.content: A meteorite recovered immediately upon its fall to Earth on July 16, 2024, is helping NASA scientists uncover new clues about ancient water, the chemical evolution of primitive asteroids, and the ingredients that may have helped make life possible throughout the early solar system.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Our World: The Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter
entry.content: Learn about the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO). As it orbits the moon for one year: LRO will collect moon temperatures, determine safe landing sites for future NASA missions, and more! Instructionally Relevant Some details are dated. This video describes NASA’s plans to launch the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO). Since this production, LRO was successfully launched […] The post Our World: The Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: How NASA’s Artemis III Lander Test Will Pave Way for Moon Landings
entry.content: Before Artemis astronauts land on the Moon’s surface in 2028, NASA will conduct the Artemis III demonstration mission in 2027, allowing teams on Earth and in orbit to practice rendezvous and docking operations between commercial human landing systems and the Orion spacecraft. Data from that mission, along with future uncrewed demonstration missions at the Moon, […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Anil Menon Launches to Space Station
entry.content: NASA astronaut candidate Anna Menon and her children watch as a Soyuz rocket launches to the International Space Station with NASA astronaut Anil Menon and Roscosmos cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, Tuesday, July 14, 2026, at the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan. The trio lifted off for the Soyuz MS-29 mission at 7:47 p.m. local […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: HWO SIG Seminar, 12 August 2026
entry.content: Title TBA Speaker TBA Seminar Connection Join the Seminar The post HWO SIG Seminar, 12 August 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Habitable Worlds Observatory SIG Seminar, 12 Aug 2026
entry.content: Habitable Worlds Observatory SIG Seminar. Details TBA The post Habitable Worlds Observatory SIG Seminar, 12 Aug 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Curiosity Finds Evidence of an Ancient Sandstorm
entry.content: Description Billions of years ago, an hours-long Martian sandstorm blew so intensely that sand ripples began to climb upon one another as they moved across the surface. These layers of sediment eventually hardened into the multilayered rocks seen in this image, which was taken by NASA’s Curiosity rover on Dec. 12, 2024, the 4,391st Martian […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: New NASA Study Says Possibly No Limit to Solar Storm Effects
entry.content: For decades, scientists have thought that there is a ceiling to how intensely Earth responds to solar storms. However, a NASA-led paper published Wednesday in Nature suggests this upper limit is an illusion. If so, it means solar storms could have far worse effects on our technology than previously thought. Observations have suggested that electric […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Provides Sweeping View of Broom Point
entry.content: Description This view looking back up at the outside lip of the 490-foot-tall (150-meter-tall) rim of Jezero Crater was taken by the Mastcam-Z instrument aboard NASA’s Perseverance on May 15, 2025, the 1,505th day, or sol, of the rover’s mission to Mars. The bright-colored rocks exposed across the slope, running from middle left to middle right of […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Viking Project
entry.content: Mission Overview NASA’s Viking Project found a place in history when it became the first U.S. mission to land a spacecraft safely on the surface of Mars and return images of the surface. Two identical spacecraft, each consisting of a lander and an orbiter, were built. Each orbiter-lander pair flew together and entered Mars orbit; […] The post Viking Project appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Astrobiology in Missions
entry.content: When humankind began to explore beyond Earth’s atmosphere, our understanding of life on Earth and the potential for life beyond our planet changed forever. Missions gathered data on locations far and wide – from the planets of our solar system to exoplanets around distant stars. Data from missions helped shape knowledge of habitability, helping astrobiologists […] The post Astrobiology in Missions appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Perseverance’s Trip to ‘Broom Point’
entry.content: Description This orbital map shows the path NASA’s Perseverance Mars rover took to get to a location the science team has dubbed the “Broom Point member,” a sequence of layered bedrock likely more than 3.9 billion years old. As planned, the rover landed inside Jezero Crater on Feb. 18, 2021. It investigated the crater’s western delta […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Station’s Newest Trio Adjusts to Mission Life Before Next Crew Departure
entry.content: 10 crew members now occupy the International Space Station following the arrival of NASA astronaut Anil Menon and Roscosmos cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina aboard the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft on Tuesday. The expanded Expedition 74 crew will stay that way until the end of the month when another three crewmates depart the orbital outpost after an eight-month space research mission.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: SSERVI CAN-5 Request for Astrophysics Panel Reviewers
entry.content: NASA’s Science Mission Directorate (SMD) and Human Spaceflight Mission Directorate (HSMD) have released the draft of the fifth Solar System Exploration Research Virtual Institute (SSERVI) Cooperative Agreement Notice (CAN-5) as NNH26ZDA013J for public comment. The post SSERVI CAN-5 Request for Astrophysics Panel Reviewers appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: LISA Science Gaps 2026 Open for Community Input
entry.content: LISA Science Gaps 2026: Open for Community Input Until 16 September 2026 The post LISA Science Gaps 2026 Open for Community Input appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Reads Record of Ancient Mars Impacts
entry.content: NASA’s Perseverance Mars rover has uncovered evidence that a 245-foot-thick (75-meter-thick) stack of ancient rock on the rim of Jezero Crater was built by repeated asteroid impacts. Referred to as the “Broom Point member” by the rover’s science team, this sequence of layered bedrock is likely more than 3.9 billion years old, making it among […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Volunteer Measures Record Louisiana Rainfall
entry.content: Join a national community of precipitation reporters providing critical data to improve scientific understanding and forecasts.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Webb Discovers Hidden Planet in Famous Star System
entry.content: Astronomers using NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope have discovered a giant planet outside our solar system, called an exoplanet, hiding within one of the most intensely studied planetary systems in our Milky Way galaxy. The young, nearby star Beta Pictoris was already known to host two giant planets: Beta Pictoris b, one of the first […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Red Sprites in the Tatacoa Desert
entry.content: Is there an angry Sith using force lightning in the Tatacoa Desert? This is not science fiction, but a red sprite with multiple streamers! Ordinary lightning occurs when thundercloud particles collide, lose their electrons, and build up negative charge at the cloud bottom
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4947-4953: Gale Crater Then and Now
entry.content: Written by Alex Innanen, Atmospheric Scientist at York University, Toronto Earth planning date: Friday, July 10, 2026 Curiosity had a successful long weekend and came into this week ready to explore some more. We’ve been moving fairly rapidly through different mapped “units,” or distinct geological areas of interest, visiting a different one at each of […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Heat Dome Broils the Western U.S.
entry.content: A ridge of high pressure fueled record-breaking temperatures in Montana, Utah, and Wyoming on July 12, 2026.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Invites Media to Serbia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
entry.content: The Republic of Serbia will sign the Artemis Accords at 5 p.m. EDT Thursday, July 16, during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington. NASA Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson will host Serbia’s Minister of Foreign Affairs Marko Đurić and U.S. State Department Assistant Secretary for Oceans and International Environmental and Scientific Affairs Wesley Brooks for the ceremony. This […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: New Trio Enters Station and Joins Expedition 74 Crew
entry.content: At 4:30 p.m. EDT, the hatch opened between the International Space Station and the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft. NASA astronaut Anil Menon and Roscosmos cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina have entered the station, where they will spend the next eight months living and working before returning to Earth in April 2027.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: SkyFall
entry.content: NASA's SkyFall mission to Mars, building on the success of the Ingenuity helicopter, will send a trio of aircraft to survey the Red Planet, uncover clues about its climate history, scout areas inaccessible to rovers, and prepare for human explorers. The post SkyFall appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Three New Crew Members Await Hatch Opening Live on NASA+
entry.content: NASA’s live hatch-opening coverage is underway on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and the agency’s YouTube channel. Hatch opening is scheduled to begin at 3:55 p.m. EDT. After, NASA astronaut Anil Menon and Roscosmos cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina will enter the International Space Station, where they will spend about eight months before returning to Earth in April 2027.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon, Crewmates Arrive at Space Station
entry.content: NASA astronaut Anil Menon, along with Roscosmos cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, arrived safely at the International Space Station Tuesday, bringing the orbiting laboratory’s crew to 10 for about the next two weeks. The trio launched aboard the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft at 10:47 a.m. EDT (7:47 p.m. local time) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Hubble Sees Crimson Cloud and Stars
entry.content: Blue and white stars shine brilliantly against a crimson background of glowing gas in this July 3, 2026, image of stellar nursery LH 95 from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope. LH 95 is a region in the Large Magellanic Cloud, a dwarf galaxy that orbits the Milky Way. Low-mass infant stars live alongside massive blue giant stars in […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Soyuz Docks to Station, Hatches Open Soon for Crew Greeting
entry.content: At 1:52 p.m. EDT, the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft docked with the International Space Station’s Prichal module. NASA astronaut Anil Menon and Roscosmos cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina launched earlier at 10:47 a.m. (7:47 p.m. Baikonur time) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan. The post Soyuz Docks to Station, Hatches Open Soon for Crew Greeting appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Space Storm Solar Sail Sentinel (S5)
entry.content: Like a sailboat turning to capture the wind, a solar sail can adjust its orbit by angling its sail. Because solar sails use the power of the Sun, they can provide constant thrust to support missions that require unique vantage points, such as those that seek to understand our Sun and its impact on Earth. […] The post Space Storm Solar Sail Sentinel (S5) appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Jets Turn Red, White, and Blue
entry.content: In honor of America’s 250th birthday, two of NASA’s most iconic aircraft got a fresh coat of red, white, and blue paint ahead of a flyover in Washington on July 4, 2026, with other NASA aircraft. An F-15 and an F/A-18 from NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, recently were repainted in […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Soyuz Arriving at Station With New Crew Live on NASA+
entry.content: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft is on track to automatically dock with the International Space Station’s Prichal module at about 1:56 p.m. EDT today. The post Soyuz Arriving at Station With New Crew Live on NASA+ appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Utah Helicopter Flights Test NASA’s DAVINCI Mission to Venus
entry.content: To prepare their spacecraft for Venus, NASA's DAVINCI scientists traveled to the most Venus-like place on Earth. The post Utah Helicopter Flights Test NASA’s DAVINCI Mission to Venus appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Soyuz Spacecraft Launches New Crew for Station Arrival Today
entry.content: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft reached orbit after its 10:47 a.m. EDT (7:47 p.m. Baikonur time) launch from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan, carrying NASA astronaut Anil Menon and Roscosmos cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina to the International Space Station.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA’s Roman Telescope Will Spot Distant Black Holes That Shred Stars
entry.content: Lee esta nota de prensa en español aquí. How do black holes at the center of galaxies form and grow over time? To answer this question, scientists need to detect and study supermassive black holes at great distances, which existed much earlier in the universe’s history. New research suggests NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: New Station Crew Counts Down to Soyuz Launch Live on NASA+
entry.content: Live launch coverage is underway on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and the agency’s YouTube channel as teams prepare for the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft launch to the International Space Station. Liftoff is scheduled for 10:47 a.m. EDT (7:47 p.m. Baikonur time) on Tuesday from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Double Lobed Asteroid Torifune
entry.content:
Why is this asteroid a double?
Earlier this month the Japanese robotic spacecraft Hayabusa2 shot past asteroid 98943 Torifune and captured pictures
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Fans of the Arctic
entry.content: Sediment eroded from ice-capped mountains splays out across a broad river valley on Russia’s Severny Island.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4941-4947: (Pin)Stripes on the Fourth of July
entry.content: Written by Deborah Padgett, MSL Operations Product Ground System Task Lead at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory Earth planning date: Thursday, July 2, 2026 Curiosity spent the week leading up to the Fourth of July holiday approaching a geologic boundary between a very smooth but somewhat sandy region and a rougher bedrock unit. Leaving the polygonal […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Study Points to Smoother Air Taxi Rides
entry.content: No one wants to get into an uncomfortable aircraft. NASA research could help the emerging industry of air taxis —small, vertical-takeoff-and-landing aircraft meant for short trips — understand the relationship between comfort and willingness to fly. That’s where NASA comes in, with data that can help identify how to plan air taxi rides that can […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s SunRISE Mission Changes Launch Vehicle to SpaceX Falcon Heavy
entry.content: NASA’s SunRISE (Sun Radio Interferometer Space Experiment) mission will launch on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida, shifting from its original ride into space aboard a United Launch Alliance Vulcan Centaur vehicle. NASA will share updated launch timing in the near future. The heliophysics mission will fly as […] The post NASA’s SunRISE Mission Changes Launch Vehicle to SpaceX Falcon Heavy appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Roman Workshop, 13-16 July 2026
entry.content: Shifting Landscapes in Astrophysics New Frontiers to Explore with Roman The post Roman Workshop, 13-16 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Artemis III Flight Hardware Stacks Up at Kennedy
entry.content: It is full steam ahead at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida as teams process and assemble flight hardware in preparation for the upcoming crewed Artemis III mission. Less than three months after the safe return of the Artemis II crew, teams began stacking the twin SLS (Space Launch System) solid rocket booster segments inside […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Expedition 74 Stays Busy and Awaits Tuesday’s New Crew Arrival
entry.content: Expedition 74 is poised to welcome three new crew members to the International Space Station who are counting down to their launch aboard a Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon
entry.content: NASA astronaut Anil Menon poses in a spacesuit for a portrait at NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, Texas on Jan. 8, 2026. Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Hubble Discovers First of Star Cluster’s Missing Black Holes
entry.content: Astronomers using archival data from Hubble and supportive observations from Webb have located their first stellar-mass black hole in the star cluster Omega Centauri.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.content: Date: July 13, 2026 - July 17, 2026 Location: University of Cambridge The post Debris Disk Connections appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.title: Wild, Scenic, and Increasingly Rusty
entry.content: Orange streams are now being spotted in hundreds of watersheds in permafrost areas throughout Alaska’s Brooks Range. The post Wild, Scenic, and Increasingly Rusty appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Galaxy NGC 474: Shells and Star Streams
entry.content:
What's happening to galaxy NGC 474?
The multiple layers of emission appear strangely complex given the relatively featureless appearance of the elliptical galaxy in less deep images
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Messier 24: Sagittarius Star Cloud
entry.content: Unlike most entries in Charles Messier's famous catalog of deep sky objects, M24 is not a bright galaxy, star cluster, or nebula
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: NASA CubeSat to Speed Technology Testing in Orbit
entry.content: The R5-S9 (Realizing Rapid, Reduced-cost high-Risk Research project Spacecraft 9) CubeSat, developed by NASA’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, lifted off at 12:12 a.m. PDT Tuesday, July 7, aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 4 East at Vandenberg Space Force Base in California as part of the Transporter-17 rideshare mission with SpaceX. […]

entry.title: Freedom 250 Flies High at NASA
entry.content: Enjoy this collection of NASA aircraft images honoring the United States' 250th birthday in 2026. The post Freedom 250 Flies High at NASA appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards

entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Calibration Instrument Records First Measurements in Space
entry.content: NASA’s Calibration Absolute Radiance and Refractivity Observatory Pathfinder, or CLARREO Pathfinder mission, reached a significant milestone this summer by achieving first light. First light showcases a mission’s ability to successfully power on and capture data, in this case detailed spectral data, validating years of preparation and pre-launch testing. This moment marks the first time the […] The post NASA Calibration Instrument Records First Measurements in Space appeared first on NASA Scienc
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
entry.content: The Zooniverse, a NASA grantee that runs the world’s largest platform for online people-powered research, has reached an extraordinary milestone: 1 billion classifications contributed by volunteers around the world. This milestone is a celebration of everyone who has marked a dip in a light curve, confirmed the presence of a moving object in a short […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Viking: 50 Years on Mars
entry.content: Only 10 years before the Viking mission launched, Mars was still just a mottled orange ball when viewed through even the best Earthbound telescopes, with few discernable features. Three NASA missions flew past in the 1960s, snagging grainy snapshots of a sliver of Mars as they hurtled by. In 1971, the Mariner 9 spacecraft arrived, […] The post Viking: 50 Years on Mars appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Earthrise, Earthset: Looking Towards Home
entry.content: Views of Earth from human & robotic explorers on their way to, around, and back home from the Moon. The post Earthrise, Earthset: Looking Towards Home appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Moonscapes
entry.content: See the Moon's mountains, craters, lava flows, and more through the "eyes" of robotic explorers like NASA's Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (LRO). The post Moonscapes appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Revealing the Invisible
entry.content: These Moon data maps reveal lunar surface temperatures, slope and elevation, subsurface rockiness, and more. The post Revealing the Invisible appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: The Moon from Earth
entry.content: Images of eclipses, supermoons, and more, captured by photographers right here on Earth. The post The Moon from Earth appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.content: POV: you're on the Moon. These are images captured by human and robotic visitors to the lunar surface. The post Views from the Lunar Surface appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.content: The first Earthrise picture snapped over the lunar horizon. Artemis II Moon photos. Decades of views from robotic explorers. Find top Moon images here. The post Moon Image Galleries appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
entry.content: NASA flight photographers capture history from a perspective few ever experience, getting a rare bird’s-eye view of the agency’s missions in action. Their photos document key NASA research and give the public a front-row seat to the work happening behind the scenes. Jim Ross, a photographer at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Beta Pictoris d
entry.content: Beta Pictoris d is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a A-type star. Its mass is 2.4 Jupiters, it takes 91 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 26 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post Beta Pictoris d appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-6019 b
entry.content: TOI-6019 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a G-type star. Its mass is 0.46880643 Jupiters, it takes 14.5 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.1212 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-6019 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TOI-2147 b
entry.content: TOI-2147 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 0.36497682 Jupiters, it takes 26.2 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.172 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TOI-2147 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Week Ends With Vein Scans, Cartilage Engineering Before New Crew Launches
entry.content: Vein scans and cartilage engineering wrapped up a busy week aboard the International Space Station. The Expedition 74 crew members also configured the orbital outpost for a new crew, tested new cooking gear, and conducted muscle measurements.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
entry.content: The waxing gibbous moon is nestled in the darkness of space in this June 26, 2026, image from the International Space Station. The space station was 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar at the time. The waxing gibbous phase comes before the full moon phase. During this time, the Moon appears brighter […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Western Moon, Eastern Sea
entry.content: The Mare Orientale, Latin for Eastern Sea, is one of the most striking large scale lunar features

entry.title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
entry.content: Radar data from the NISAR satellite show that La Guaira and nearby areas experienced significant ground displacement from the June 2026 temblors.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Video: The LAST Eclipse in History
entry.content: We are in the Golden Age of Solar Eclipses, but only for the moment. In fact, we may already be past peak solar eclipse conditions, and it's all downhill from here. The post Video: The LAST Eclipse in History appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Video: Which Planet Has the Best Eclipse?
entry.content: Solar eclipses don't just happen here on earth - moons of other planets also pass between those planets and the Sun, resulting in various types of solar eclipses across the solar system. The post Video: Which Planet Has the Best Eclipse? appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Helio and You: June 2026
entry.content: As the only state to lie partly within the Arctic Circle, Alaska experiences a wide range of extreme conditions. But with these extremes comes the chance to conduct science vital to understanding the Sun-Earth system, including launching sounding rockets to study solar flares, auroras, and other phenomena. This article explores the ways that scientists use […] The post Helio and You: June 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Anil Menon Launch to Space Station
entry.content: NASA astronaut Anil Menon will launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on Tuesday, July 14, accompanied by cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina, where they will join the Expedition 74 crew advancing scientific research. Menon, Dubrov, and Kikina will lift off at 10:47 a.m. EDT (7:47 p.m. Baikonur time) […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: AGN SIG Spotlight Series, 21 July 2026
entry.content: Our Spotlight Series highlights recent advances in AGN science, with a strong emphasis on participation from early-career researchers, and includes plenty of time for community discussion following the presentations. The post AGN SIG Spotlight Series, 21 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Space Telescope Maps Magnetic Fields of ‘Lighthouse’ Pulsar
entry.content: For the first time, scientists have used NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) to directly measure the magnetic fields of PSR J1101−6101, a pulsar located within what is often referred to as the Lighthouse Nebula. The results provide new insight into the structure of some of the most extreme objects in the cosmos, as NASA […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s Roman Launch Preparations Proceed
entry.content: With less than two months until the launch of NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, engineers at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida are preparing the observatory for its trip to space. Inside the Payload Hazardous Servicing Facility, technicians used large cranes to carefully lift Roman and place it onto a specialized work platform, known […] The post NASA’s Roman Launch Preparations Proceed appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Neurology Studies and Crew Departure Preps Fill Station Schedule
entry.content: Neurology studies and crew departure preparations filled the schedule aboard the International Space Station on Thursday. Expedition 74 also kept up the maintenance of the orbital lab replacing electronics and GPS gear and organizing cargo.

entry.title: NASA’s Dragonfly Clears Key Tests as Titan Rotorcraft Takes Shape
entry.content: NASA’s Dragonfly is starting to look less like a collection of spacecraft parts and more like the rotorcraft that will fly across the surface of Titan, Saturn’s hazy moon. The mission reached a major milestone on June 29, when the Dragonfly team at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel, Maryland, delivered the […] The post NASA’s Dragonfly Clears Key Tests as Titan Rotorcraft Takes Shape appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
entry.content: This close-up view shows fragments of sulfur crystals — the first ever seen on the Red Planet. The crystals were found after NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover happened to drive over a rock and crush it on May 30, 2024. Several days later, Curiosity used a camera on the end of its robotic arm to take […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Scientists Take to Air and Space to Study Arctic Sea Ice
entry.content: This month, engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California are testing a spacecraft sensor that will help measure how quickly Arctic sea ice is disappearing. And while that instrument won’t launch for another year, scientists started preparing for its use during a recent field campaign in the Canadian wilderness. Researchers spent two weeks […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA Small Satellite Could Make Global Positioning More Precise
entry.content: GRITSS will demonstrate the feasibility of a new technique for improving precision navigation and geodetic measurements.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: The Red Glow of the Cosmic Bat Nebula
entry.content: This Cosmic Bat wishes you a happy Summerween! This mid-year celebration of Halloween transcends hemispheres, even though summer in the Northern hemisphere is winter in the South
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Super Typhoon Bavi
entry.content: The third category 5 tropical cyclone of 2026 crossed the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam before continuing toward Asia.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
entry.content: Story by Keri Moskowitz, Gulf of Maine Research Institute For the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation, or Sipayik, the ocean has always been a teacher. Situated in what is known as Downeast Maine, along the shores of Passamaquoddy Bay, generations of Indigenous people have lived along the coast, learning from the tides, the land, and their […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Crew Works Health Checks, CubeSat Maintenance, and Soyuz Seat Checks
entry.content: Health checks and CubeSat hardware maintenance were the prime research objectives aboard the International Space Station on Wednesday. The Expedition 74 crew members also stowed spacewalking gear, transferred cargo, and checked seats in a Soyuz spacecraft.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Small Steps, Giant Leaps Podcast Episode 177: Transformative Aeronautics
entry.content: A NASA research program collaborates with universities to revolutionize the way we design, build, and operate aircraft. Angela Surgenor, deputy program manager of the Transformative Aeronautics Concepts Program, explains. The post Small Steps, Giant Leaps Podcast Episode 177: Transformative Aeronautics appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: You’re Always Surrounded by Neutrinos!
entry.content: This second, as you’re reading these words, trillions of tiny particles are hurtling toward you! No, you don’t need to brace yourself. They’re passing through you right now. And now. And now. These particles are called neutrinos, and they’re both everywhere in the cosmos and also extremely hard to find. Neutrinos are fundamental particles, like […] The post You’re Always Surrounded by Neutrinos! appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Awards Contracts for Mars Advanced Surface Mobility Technology
entry.content: July 8, 2026 C.13 NASA Awards Contracts for Mars Advanced Surface Mobility Technology NASA has selected seven companies for contract awards under the Mars Exploration Program’s Science Transport and Robotic Innovation for Deployment and Exploration, or STRIDE, initiative to advance next-generation commercial robotic surface mobility for future Mars exploration. The STRIDE awards will support the […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
entry.content: This image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope showcases Messier 3 (M3), one of the Milky Way galaxy’s most massive globular clusters, or spherical collections of gravitationally bound stars. Globular clusters are made up of ancient stars that formed at roughly the same time from the same cloud of gas, giving those stars similar ages. Around 150 known globular clusters are […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Cottonwood Fire Chars Utah
entry.content: The blaze burned more than 150 square miles and swept through parts of a ski resort.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.content: Scientist-astronaut Harrison H. Schmitt is photographed working beside a huge boulder on the Moon. The post Harrison “Jack” Schmitt, Busy on the Moon (Apollo 17) appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.content: The primary goal of this collaboration was to support and validate science data products from NASA's recently launched Plankton, Aerosol, Cloud, ocean Ecosystem (PACE) satellite mission The post Keeping PACE with Ocean Change appeared first on NASA Science .

entry.title: More Cartilage, Blood Pressure Studies on Station as Crew Swap Preps Begin
entry.content: Cartilage engineering and blood pressure studies to improve health on Earth and in space continued aboard the International Space Station on Tuesday. The Expedition 74 crew members also serviced an experimental module, configured a temporary sleep station, and began preparing for the return to Earth of three crewmates.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
entry.content: NASA ceremonially transferred ownership of about 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center’s Greenbelt, Maryland, campus Tuesday to the adjoining Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The property, formerly known as NASA Goddard’s Area 400, is now part of the largest block of unfragmented forest between […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
entry.content: Following its longest hibernation period ever of nearly a year, NASA’s New Horizons spacecraft has emerged in good health and is ready to begin transmitting science data gathered in the distant Kuiper Belt far beyond Pluto. On June 23, flight controllers at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory (APL) in Laurel, Maryland, confirmed New Horizons, […] The post NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
entry.content: In this photograph, the Artemis II crew participates in the dedication of the Apollo 14 Moon tree at the Lunar Receiving Park at NASA’s Johnson Space Center on June 25, 2026. This tree is a second-generation Apollo Moon tree of the loblolly pine species. The original Apollo Moon trees were grown from seeds carried aboard […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: July 2026 Satellite Puzzler
entry.content: Your challenge is to tell us the location of the satellite image and why it is interesting.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s COFFIES Science Center Makes Breakthrough on Solar Enigma
entry.content: Researchers working with one of NASA’s DRIVE (Diversify, Realize, Integrate, Venture, Educate) Science Centers are closer to unraveling a long-standing solar mystery surrounding the extreme thinness of the Sun’s tachocline layer, a region critical for creating space weather. The COFFIES (Consequences Of Fields and Flows in the Interior and Exterior of the Sun) center enabled […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NGC 6188: Dragons of Ara
entry.content:
Where can you find dragons fighting in the night sky?
In the southern constellation of the Altar: Ara
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: The World Cup From 250 Miles Up
entry.content: Over the years, astronauts aboard the International Space Station have photographed several of the cities hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: HoRS3S
entry.content: Date: July 6, 2026 - July 10, 2026 Location: Granada, Spain The post HoRS3S appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Cartilage Engineering, Blood Pressure Studies Kick Off Week Aboard Station
entry.content: Cartilage engineering and cardiac research to advance human health on and off the Earth topped Monday’s research schedule aboard the International Space Station. The Expedition 74 crew members also continued spacesuit maintenance and cargo operations aboard the orbital outpost. The post Cartilage Engineering, Blood Pressure Studies Kick Off Week Aboard Station appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Telescope Milestone: NASA’s Roman Moves Vertical Ahead of Processing
entry.content: NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has completed important prelaunch milestones as it prepares to launch nine months ahead of schedule. Engineers at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida raised Roman from horizontal to vertical, signaling preparations are moving forward for upcoming inspections, functional testing, and integration work. Technicians transferred the observatory to NASA […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
entry.content: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover featuring his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. For 250 years, America has pushed the boundaries of what’s possible. From the earliest days of exploration, to the first steps on the Moon […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: ESA’s Euclid Space Telescope Finds Universe’s Most Ancient Quasars
entry.content: Led by ESA (European Space Agency) with contributions from NASA, the Euclid space telescope has discovered 31 of the oldest quasars ever documented. In fact, two are the oldest ever observed, dating back to the universe’s infancy, when it was just 5% of its current age. A new study details the results in the journal Astronomy & Astrophysics. A quasar occurs when immense amounts of gas and dust […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: XR SIG Seminar, 10 July 2026
entry.content: High Angular Resolution X-ray Imager (Hi-ReX) SAG Report on Extragalactic and Extreme Gravity Science.Speaker: Kimberly Weaver, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center The post XR SIG Seminar, 10 July 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA Seeks Industry Input on Second Phase of Commercial Space Stations
entry.content: On Monday, NASA released a draft Request for Proposals (RFP) seeking feedback from American companies on the next phase of its commercial space stations strategy, aimed at ensuring a seamless transition of activities in low Earth orbit from the International Space Station. “NASA’s review reflects what we’ve been hearing from industry throughout this process. Industry […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: NASA’s CAPSTONE Completes Extended Mission Testing Lunar Technologies
entry.content: As NASA prepares for a sustained human presence on the Moon, missions will increasingly require spacecraft that can navigate and communicate without a direct connection to Earth. NASA’s Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment, or CAPSTONE, validated and advanced these capabilities. Designed to test and validate technologies in lunar orbit, CAPSTONE launched […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Strong Flare Erupts from Sun
entry.content: The Sun emitted a strong solar flare, peaking at 4:41 p.m. ET on July 4. NASA’s Solar Dynamics Observatory, which watches the Sun constantly, captured an image of the event. Solar flares are powerful bursts of energy. Flares and solar eruptions can impact radio communications, electric power grids, navigation signals, and pose risks to spacecraft […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: NASA Webb Uncovers Unusual Galaxy Shaped by Cosmic Collision
entry.content: In new images from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope to celebrate its fourth science anniversary, a familiar galaxy transforms into something far richer, and far more complex, than ever seen before. Webb’s unprecedented sensitivity across near- and mid-infrared wavelengths cuts through the thick lanes of dust that obscure Centaurus A’s center in visible light, showing […]
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Dueling Bands over the Atacama Desert
entry.content:
What are these two bands in the sky?
The more commonly seen band is on the left and is the central band of our Milky Way galaxy
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: IAUS 407: Origins 2026
entry.content: Date: July 6, 2026 - July 11, 2026 Location: Paris, France The post IAUS 407: Origins 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
entry.content: Using satellite data, researchers connected harmful algal blooms with warm water and low water levels at one of Colorado's largest reservoirs. The post Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: Saturn's Iapetus: Painted Moon
entry.content:
What has happened to Saturn's moon Iapetus?
Vast sections of this strange world are dark as coal, while others are as bright as snow
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: SPIE Astronomical Telescopes + Instrumentation 2026
entry.content: Date: July 5, 2026 - July 10, 2026 Location: Copenhagen, Denmark The post SPIE Astronomical Telescopes + Instrumentation 2026 appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
entry.content: Red, white, and blue stars glitter like a sparkler being waved on a dark night in this new image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.
entry.dc:creator: NASA

entry.title: Pathfinder on Mars
entry.content: On July 4th, 1997, using its own array of fireworks, a parachute, and a cocoon of airbags, the Mars Pathfinder spacecraft bounced like a giant beach ball at least 15 times before it came to rest on the surface of Mars at 10:07 AM Pacific Daylight Time
entry.dc:creator: APOD

entry.title: Teams Make Contact With Spacecraft Set to Boost NASA’s Swift
entry.content: Teams have successfully established communications with Katalyst Space’s robotic servicing spacecraft LINK.
entry.dc:creator: NASA Blogs

entry.title: Hubble Celebrates Nation’s 250th Birthday
entry.content: NASA's Hubble celebrates the nation's 250th birthday with new images and more! The post Hubble Celebrates Nation’s 250th Birthday appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: TIC 150070085 b
entry.content: TIC 150070085 b is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a F-type star. Its mass is 12.8 Earths, it takes 10.5 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.098439381424078 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post TIC 150070085 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 126105 b
entry.content: HD 126105 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 1.67 Jupiters, it takes 1.4 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 1.36 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 126105 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 127195 c
entry.content: HD 127195 c is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 0.65 Jupiters, it takes 2.3 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 1.9162400865645 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 127195 c appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 127195 b
entry.content: HD 127195 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 0.67 Jupiters, it takes 1.5 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 1.4210244419444 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 127195 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 125136 b
entry.content: HD 125136 b is a gas giant exoplanet that orbits a K-type star. Its mass is 2.26 Jupiters, it takes 2.3 years to complete one orbit of its star, and is 2.0319497862624 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 125136 b appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science

entry.title: HD 190360 d
entry.content: HD 190360 d is a Neptune-like exoplanet that orbits a G-type star. Its mass is 11.9 Earths, it takes 88.7 days to complete one orbit of its star, and is 0.3886 AU from its star. Its discovery was announced in 2026. The post HD 190360 d appeared first on NASA Science .
entry.dc:creator: NASA Science
